direction_head.model: StandardScaler plus multinomial logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
direction_head.representation: mean and population SD over g=2..16 for each of 27 non-depth coordinates; identical to the external-panel depth grid
direction_head.score_interpretation: uncalibrated out-of-distribution score; not a probability or posterior
direction_head.training_selection: all positive canonical replicates at every migration rate

gate_head.model: StandardScaler plus binary logistic regression (lbfgs, C=1)
gate_head.representation: selected depth rows plus across-depth mean of 27 non-depth coordinates
gate_head.score_interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not the canonical 243-D gate probability
gate_head.training_target: positive migration with rate >=2.5e-4 versus weak positives plus control

guardrail: candidate-class-A same-data sensitivity only; no panel is independent, gold truth, or accuracy-eligible, and severe OOD outputs abstain

outcome_summary.interpretation: one same-data, mixed-direction Harpagifer comparison repeated over two sample scopes and two locus-ascertainment filters; panel counts are not accuracy trials

adjudication.severe_OOD_rule: max(direction RMS-z, gate RMS-z) > 10; heuristic, not calibrated support

external_expectation.direction_basis: dominant same-data BayesAss estimate reported by the source study; not a clean one-edge history or external label
external_expectation.label_source_reuse: the source paper inferred clusters and migration on the same 2,993 GBS SNPs; no held-out loci or samples define the candidate label
external_expectation.locus_filter_variant: both alleles called across the complete three-population panel
external_expectation.tree_contract_status: operational three-cluster order, not a rooted species tree; source study argued for one evolutionary unit

filter_contract.cap_method: fixed-seed reservoir over eligible loci, restored to source order
filter_contract.filter: PASS or dot (sources are author-filtered releases)
filter_contract.polymorphism: both REF and ALT must be called across every declared three-population panel
filter_contract.variant: single-base REF and single-base ALT; no multiallelic ALT

iid_locus_bootstrap.guardrail: fixed-sample conditional sensitivity only; all source CHROM values are 0 and no linkage map exists, so independence is unverified and this is not chromosome-block uncertainty
iid_locus_bootstrap.method: naive IID resampling of released GBS loci

model_free_comparator.interpretation: sample-frequency geometry only; projection is reference-flip invariant but is not bounded ancestry or temporal direction. The finite-copy f3 correction assumes independent binomial called-copy sampling and is not generally unbiased. The prespecified 0.95 diagnostic threshold is retained even when it yields zero loci and is not tuned post hoc.

population_order.tree_contract_status: operational three-cluster order, not a rooted species tree; source study argued for one evolutionary unit

simulation_feature_shift.reference: StandardScaler fitted to 2,700 positive regen_full summaries on the identical g=2..16 grid

simulation_gate.interpretation: depth-matched uncalibrated OOD score; not a probability or posterior

simulation_head.interpretation: uncalibrated OOD scores; not probabilities or posteriors

published_evidence.biological_guardrail: the source paper rejected incipient speciation and described one evolutionary unit despite three SNP clusters; P3 is a population cluster, not a species outgroup
published_evidence.candidate_direction: source-study BayesAss means reported 18.8% north-to-south Patagonia versus 2.03% reciprocal migration
published_evidence.method_guardrail: BayesAss used 10,000 iterations with 10% burn-in in the source paper; reported fractions are not per-generation msprime migration rates
published_evidence.newer_related_work: Bernal-Duran et al. 2024 (10.1111/mec.17360) studies H. antarcticus with a different 143-sample/20,778-SNP Western Antarctic Peninsula dataset; it informs geographic-connectivity nuisance but does not update or validate this label
published_evidence.other_reported_edges: north-to-Falklands/Malvinas 1.15% and south-to-Falklands/Malvinas 2.54%; reverse island-to-Patagonia estimates were negligible and not plotted

runtime.process_priority: BelowNormal requested at runner start on Windows

source.ascertainment_guardrails: ApeKI GBS/UNEAK neutral release, source MAF >=0.05 and site call-rate >=0.75, removal of loci with HWE deviation in at least 60% of localities after FDR, removal of 68 Bayescan differentiated candidates from 3,061 SNPs, same-data clustering and migration labels, reconstructed site membership, and unknown linkage

sample_missingness_le_0_25.selection: prespecified sample-level sensitivity retaining missing-GT fraction <=0.25

sample_missingness_sensitivity.guardrail: not an outcome-based or direction-model-based sample filter, but strongly group-reweighting: removes 10/50 P1, 1/43 P2, and 0/25 P3

mapping_audit.guardrail: the grouping is a strong order-and-count reconstruction used for sensitivity analysis, not an author-supplied individual crosswalk
known_discrepancies: Supplementary Table S1 SNP counts [2, 14, 15, 7, 11, 15, 15, 13, 25] sum to 117 and report TEM n=2, while the VCF has 118 samples and three leading TEM-like columns.
Main-text Table 3 sample sizes [3, 13, 13, 13, 12, 19, 14, 14, 27] sum to 128 and therefore do not crosswalk one-to-one to the 118-column released VCF.
The block record enumerates only VCF-positive reconstructed site blocks, not every collection locality; Supplementary Table S1 omits FM and sequence tables use inconsistent site acronyms.

site_blocks.provenance_note: VCF has three leading samples although Supplementary Table S1 reports SNP n=2
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
contiguous VCF-order reconstruction from Supplementary Table S1
Hpal-prefixed Hookers Point samples form the final VCF block (the paper's M1 cluster)

source_vcf_contract.allele_orientation_guardrail: the Tassel header states that the reference allele is unknown and the major allele was encoded as REF; REF must not be interpreted as ancestral
source_vcf_contract.linkage_guardrail: all released loci use CHROM=0 and no reference assembly or linkage map is supplied; physical independence cannot be verified

known_discrepancies: Supplementary Table S1 reports TEM SNP n=2 and total n=117, while the released VCF has three leading TEM-like samples and 118 samples total.
Main-text Table 3 site counts sum to 128 and cannot define membership in the 118-column released VCF.
The nine-row block record enumerates VCF-positive reconstructed blocks, not every collection site; the supplement omits FM and uses inconsistent site acronyms in sequence tables.

supplement_file.guardrail: The live item's 2026 modification is metadata-only; file ID, bytes, MD5, and version remain unchanged.

archive_wrapper_observation.guardrail: repeated official downloads produced generated ZIP wrappers with different member timestamps and SHA-256 values; only the byte-identical inner VCF is canonical and pinned
